core_model.structure_summary: Second-order phase variables on the nodes of a weighted graph, each with inertia, linear damping, a constant node-specific drive, and pairwise coupling through the sine of phase differences weighted by the graph's edge weights, exhibiting partial synchronization that depends on topology.

variables.meaning: phase angle attached to each node of the graph
weighted adjacency matrix whose entries follow heavy-tailed distributions

deterministic_skeleton.summary: Coupled second-order ODEs for node phases on a weighted graph with linear damping, constant heterogeneous drives, and sinusoidal pairwise coupling in phase differences.

notes: The paper combines (a) simulation of the second-order phase-coupled oscillator system on empirical graphs and (b) extensive empirical statistical analysis (heavy-tailed/power-law fits of parameters and edge weights, q-Gaussian increment distributions via superstatistics, community-structure measures). The dynamical system is taken as the core model since the synchronization results are about it; the statistical fits characterize its inputs and outputs. scale_invariance and phase_transition tags reflect the heavy-tailed parameter distributions and the synchronization transition/chimera behavior central to the results.
